name: Safiyya bint Hussain
title: Wandering Child
shortDescription: One of the Master of Martyrs' (AS) young daughters who wandered in the desert during the Kufan army's attack on the tents and suffered captivity.
fullStory:
Safiyya bint al-Hussain is another daughter of the Master of Martyrs (AS) who is mentioned in some Maqatil as one of the children present in Karbala.

Alongside the other children in the caravan, she witnessed the water being blocked from the tents and the successive martyrdom of her companions and relatives. The peak of tragedy for this innocent child occurred when Shimr ordered the attack on the tents.

The young girls' earrings were ruthlessly pulled from their ears and their clothes were plundered. In that chaos, Safiyya fled to the desert with the other women and spent the night of Sham-e-Ghariban (the night of the strangers) in fear and terror in the dark desert.

She was then taken as a prisoner of war by ruthless soldiers like Zahr ibn Qays and Khawli, and traveled the harsh route from Karbala to Kufa and Kufa to Sham in captivity.
quote: (Condition of the children:) Orphans and widows being driven in shackles...
source: Mawsu'at Kalimat al-Imam al-Hussain / Bihar al-Anwar
